current_knowledge: Knows the product better than the judges do.
May want to show every feature.
May not have converted the rubric into slide choices.
stakes: Needs judges to understand the value quickly.
Needs to prove the hard part without drowning the audience in implementation detail.
Needs a fallback if the live demo breaks.
success_criteria: Can turn judging criteria into a slide plan.
Can decide what belongs in the main deck versus backup.

likely_questions.question: What should we show if we only have three minutes?
How much technical detail should we include?
What if the live demo fails?
How do we know if the idea itself is weak?

objections.objection: Different hackathons have different judging criteria.
A generic demo deck cannot prove a specific project works.

audience_pov_critique.what_they_care_about: What to show, what to cut, and how to make judges trust the demo.
audience_pov_critique.what_will_feel_unproven: Any claim that a format guarantees winning or proves a project without project-specific evidence.
audience_pov_critique.where_deck_sounds_like_us_not_them: Too much discussion of slide-generation internals instead of judged demo needs.
audience_pov_critique.required_reframe: Tie every slide to a judge action: understand, score, inspect proof, ask a better question, or remember the project.
